mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward test shows that month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months, or the mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate persistently above 7.5% indicates substantial labor market slack, which anchors expectations of prolonged accommodative monetary policy and supports equity risk appetites, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 7.5%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of triggered months, or the mean forward return under this threshold is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls indicates coordinated strength in consumer demand and labor income, which amplifies earnings expectations beyond the individual contributions of either series and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and PAYEMS changes shows a t-statistic below 1.5 in predicting 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window, or a simple additive model outperforms the interaction model in out-of-sample R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis zero lower bound regime, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal successful transmission of accommodative monetary policy into credit-sensitive real demand, lifting cyclical earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, positive month-over-month changes in HOUST do not yield significantly positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (mean return <= 0 or t-stat < 1.5) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation exceeding 30 basis points forces a rapid repricing of near-term monetary tightening risk, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter print in real GDP signals an economic contraction that raises recession probabilities and triggers earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as risk premia expand.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is negative, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of triggered quarters, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with a low but increasing federal funds rate signals stagflationary monetary tightening, where the central bank restricts policy into a weakening labor market; this joint dynamic compresses earnings expectations and discount rates, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint interaction of rising UNRATE and rising FEDFUNDS fails to produce a t-statistic below -1.5 in predicting 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the directional accuracy of the negative signal falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate wage income and consumer spending power, which lifts top-line revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
